Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Specially Appearing Defts To: 1) Quash Defective Service Of Process; 2) Declare Pltf A Vexatious Litigant; 3) Prohibit Pltf From Filing New Actions Wihtout Leave Of Presiding Judge; And 4) Require Pltf To Furnish Security; Request For Judicial Notice
Off Calendar Nov-19-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Andrew Gschwind, Esq., Deputy City Attorney, 415-554-3973 for defendant and moving party City and County of San Francisco

RULING:
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING:
DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AND LAURELLE PERRY'S Motion Of Specially Appearing Defendants To: 1) Quash Defective Service Of Process; 2) Declare Plaintiff A Vexatious Litigant; 3) Prohibit Plaintiff From Filing New Actions Without Leave Of Presiding Judge; And 4) Require Plaintiff To Furnish Security; Request For Judicial Notice.
- The motion to quash service of summons is granted. Ms. Perry has not been personally served nor served by substituted service. Plaintiff has not served either the Board of Supervisors or the Mayor's Office. The Health Services is not an entity to be served.
- Plaintiff is declared a vexatious litigant under CCP 391(b)(1). Measured from the time the motion for security is filed, Plaintiff has had more than five cases determined against her in the past seven years. Stolz v. Bank of America (1993) 15Cal.App.4th 217.
- Plaintiff's requirement to furnish security is denied without prejudice. May be addressed in subsequent hearings.
- The request to preclude Plaintiff from filing further suits is denied without prejudice to be brought before the Presiding Judge. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600. (302/PJM/AY)

Default Prove-Up.
Off Calendar Nov-06-2007 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled for Default Prove-Up Hearing this day.
Dept. 218, Comm. Sue Kaplan, Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha, CSR: Trish Abalos.
Plaintiff: Janet Pickens-Stanford, present in pro per, was sworn and examined.
Matter is ordered off calendar, per written Order granted on Oct. 25, 2007 by Dept. 302, Judge Stewart, setting aside the default as to Defendant: Dept. of Human Services.
Defendants' Motion to declare Plaintiff as Vexatious Litigant is set for hearing in Dept. 302 on Nov. 19, 2007.
